commit.message:
fix: enable token store with blob SAS for B2C, unify loginParameters

- B2C token store now uses blob storage (token-store-sas secret) — without
  token store, EasyAuth callback returns 401 (can't persist auth state)
- loginParameters: both B2B and B2C now use response_type=code (removes
  unnecessary conditional)
- B2C nonce validation remains disabled (CIAM omits nonce from id_tokens)

commit.message:
fix(zsh): force emacs key bindings to restore Ctrl-A with EDITOR=nvim

zsh automatically switches to vi mode when EDITOR contains 'vim'/'nvim'.
bindkey -e explicitly sets emacs bindings so Ctrl-A (move to line start)
works regardless of the EDITOR value.

commit.message:
test(cli): add 127 comprehensive e2e tests for wheelctl (#171)

Add crates/cli/tests/cli_comprehensive_e2e_tests.rs with 127 new
end-to-end tests across 15 test modules covering:

- Telemetry command execution (probe, capture, supported/unsupported games)
- Profile validation edge cases (empty, binary, huge, unicode, nested errors)
- JSON structural consistency (well-formed objects, success keys)
- Error message actionability (suggestions, context, exit codes)
- Help text snapshots for 16 leaf-level subcommands
- Error output snapshots for 10 parse-error scenarios
- Workflow integration (create/validate/edit/export/import roundtrip)
- Edge cases (long args, special chars, repeated flags, empty strings)
- Output format parity (JSON vs human for same commands)
- Version format validation
- Global flag interactions (--json with -v, --endpoint)
- Plugin, game, diag, and safety command execution

All tests follow project conventions:
- Result-returning tests (no unwrap/expect)
- assert_cmd for binary invocation
- insta for snapshot testing
- tempfile for filesystem isolation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(fuzz): add edge-case fuzz targets (#193)

* feat(fuzz): add edge-case fuzz targets for seed, label, factory, negative fixtures, and JWK serialization

Add five new fuzz targets:

- fuzz_seed_edge_cases: empty/zero/0xFF/long seeds

- fuzz_label_edge_cases: empty/unicode/long/special-char labels

- fuzz_factory_stress: rapid create/destroy with random seeds

- fuzz_negative_fixture_edge: PEM/DER corruption boundary cases

- fuzz_jwk_serialization: JWK building with varied key types/configs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(fuzz): avoid false positive in seed debug redaction check

Short hex strings (e.g. 'ed' from 0xed) can match substrings of the
debug format 'Seed(**redacted**)'. Only assert on inputs >= 8 chars.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

fix(tests): anchor peak_times test to avoid hour-boundary flakiness

Sessions inserted at 'now - 5h + i*5min' could straddle an hour boundary,
splitting 3 sessions across 2 buckets and making peak_times[0].sessions == 2
instead of 3. Anchoring to 'replace(minute=0)' ensures all 3 sessions are
in the same hour bucket.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[Repo Assist] Migrate theme colors to CSS `light-dark()` function (#1059)

* Use CSS light-dark() for theme color variables

Replace the separate [data-theme=dark] block of variable overrides with
light-dark(light-val, dark-val) syntax in :root, so the browser resolves
colors automatically based on the active color-scheme.

- Add color-scheme: light dark to :root (enabling automatic system-preference
  following when no explicit theme is chosen)
- Replace 25 color variables that differed between light/dark with light-dark()
- Reduce [data-theme=dark] to only color-scheme: dark (activates dark values)
- Add [data-theme=light] { color-scheme: light } to support explicit light
  override when OS is dark
- Simplify fsdocs-theme-set-dark.js: apply saved localStorage preference early
  (removes the now-redundant prefers-color-scheme JS check, since CSS handles
  the system default automatically)
- Keep [data-theme=dark] ::backdrop rule unchanged (opacity cannot use light-dark())

Closes #1004

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: trigger CI checks

* Update docs

commit.message:
fix: resume playback via MPRIS so MA is initiator and group sync is preserved (#47)

When a grouped player's play button is pressed, send MPRIS Play instead of
IPC cmd so Music Assistant is the initiator and can re-establish group sync.
Sending play directly to individual clients breaks the group in MA.

- Add play_via_mpris() to mpris.py — sends MPRIS Play to sendspin instances,
  deduplicating by Identity to avoid double-triggers
- /api/pause_all: action='play' now uses MPRIS Play (one rep per group)
- /api/pause: action='play' now uses MPRIS Play for all players

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(key-service): restore replace_existing to UploadPreKeysRequest (field 11)

Regression: replace_existing was added in 07e9895 (field 4) to handle
stale OTPK pool recovery but was lost in bd99ed9 (merge main→develop)
because main didn't have the commit yet.

Restored on field 11 (field 4 is now reserved, as required by proto
backward-compatibility rules). Server logic also restored:
- upload_prekeys() now takes replace_existing: bool
- When true: atomically DELETEs all existing OTPKs before inserting new batch
- Default false = backward-compatible additive upload (normal replenishment)

iOS/Android: use replace_existing=true in OtpkReplenishmentService for
session init failure and heal exhaustion recovery scenarios, as before.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add Labs 076-083 + enrichment notes to existing labs

8 NEW LABS based on 2026 trends research:

Lab 076: Microsoft Agent Framework (L200) — SK+AutoGen unified into MAF.
  Migration matrix (15 features), Agent Skills, Graph Workflows, DevUI.
Lab 077: AG-UI Protocol (L200) — Agent-to-User interaction protocol.
  Completes MCP+A2A+AG-UI trilogy. 12 event types analyzed.
Lab 078: Foundry Local (L100) — Run AI models offline, free.
  8 models compared (phi-4-mini to mistral-small). OpenAI-compatible API.
Lab 079: Deep Research Agents (L300) — Multi-step knowledge synthesis.
  Planner→Researcher→Writer→Reviewer pipeline, 14-step trace.
Lab 080: MarkItDown + MCP (L200) — Document ingestion for agents.
  PDF/Word/Excel/PPTX→Markdown. 12 file conversions analyzed.
Lab 081: Agentic Coding Tools (L100) — Claude Code vs Copilot CLI.
  10 coding tasks compared across tools.
Lab 082: Agent Guardrails (L300) — NeMo + Azure Content Safety.
  15 guardrail tests: jailbreak, PII, topic control, content safety.
Lab 083: Multi-Modal RAG (L300) — Images, tables, charts in documents.
  15 chunks comparing text-only vs multimodal retrieval scores.

ENRICHMENTS TO EXISTING LABS:

- Labs 014, 023, 034 (SK): Added MAF migration warning admonition
- Lab 040 (AutoGen): Added MAF migration warning admonition
- Labs 054-055 (A2A): Added AG-UI protocol reference tip
- Lab 015 (Ollama): Added Foundry Local cross-reference tip

Total: 83 labs, 8 enriched existing labs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

all 7 e2e test files touched by PR #329.

Command::cargo_bin() in favour of the cargo_bin_cmd!() macro which
requires CARGO_BIN_EXE_* at compile time (needs copybook-cli as

commit.message:
fix(tests): strip ANSI escape codes from CliRunner output in CI

GitHub Actions sets FORCE_COLOR=1 which causes typer/rich to render option
names with per-character ANSI styling (e.g. '--limit' becomes
'-\x1b[0m\x1b[1;36m-limit'), breaking plain-string assertions.
Monkey-patching CliRunner.invoke to strip ANSI codes (via stdout_bytes) fixes
all affected tests without modifying any individual test file.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:

Add 80 platform-independent tests covering:
- HID trait implementation correctness (VirtualDevice contract)

commit.message:
Add comprehensive schema evolution tests for racing-wheel-schemas (#166)

* Add comprehensive schema evolution tests for racing-wheel-schemas

Add 155 tests across 14 modules covering schema evolution, migration,
and backward compatibility in the schemas crate:

- Domain type roundtrip serialization (TorqueNm, Degrees, DeviceId, etc.)
- Entity type roundtrip serialization (DeviceCapabilities, Profile, etc.)
- Telemetry type roundtrip (NormalizedTelemetry, TelemetryValue, etc.)
- Backward compatibility (old JSON without optional fields, minimal proto)
- Forward compatibility (unknown fields handled gracefully)
- Validation (required fields, constraints, domain rejection rules)
- Default value stability (TelemetryFlags, NormalizedTelemetry, configs)
- Enum stability (serialized string representations, i32 repr values)
- Proto/JSON consistency (proto messages match JSON schema semantics)
- Serialization snapshots (exact serialized forms of key types)
- Migration tests (SchemaVersion parsing, v0->v1 migration, backup)
- Config/profile roundtrip (ProfileValidator, field preservation)
- Proto backward compatibility (empty messages, all proto types roundtrip)
- Edge cases (negative gear, NaN handling, normalization, skip_serializing)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: narrow device_id proptest to ASCII-only invalid chars

DeviceId::from_str uses Rust's is_alphanumeric() which is Unicode-aware,
so chars like À are valid. Narrow the proptest regex to only use characters
that are never alphanumeric in any Unicode category.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: eliminate Azure auth timeout and duplicate npm pack call in package-integration tests (#49)

* Initial plan

* perf: reduce package-integration test execution time

- Cache `npm pack --dry-run --json` result with `beforeAll` to avoid
  running the expensive command twice (once per test)
- Pass explicit `--config <nonexistent-path>` in "run without config"
  tests so they fail immediately with ENOENT instead of waiting for
  DefaultAzureCredential to exhaust all auth providers (~15s per test)
- Remove now-unnecessary `{ timeout: 20_000 }` per-test overrides

Co-authored-by: Meir017 <9786571+Meir017@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Meir017 <9786571+Meir017@users.noreply.github.com>

test(engine): add comprehensive filter pipeline tests (#169)

Add 75 tests covering the RT force-feedback filter pipeline:

1. Individual filter behavior (reconstruction, friction, damper, inertia,
   notch, lowpass, slew rate, torque cap, curve, bumpstop, hands-off)
2. Filter chain composition (multi-filter series, full pipeline order)
3. Boundary conditions (zero input, max input, NaN/Inf safety)
4. Determinism (bit-exact replay, reset-then-replay equivalence)
5. Frequency response (Nyquist attenuation, notch rejection, lowpass rolloff)
6. Parameter validation (out-of-range rejection, config hashing)
7. RT constraints (zero-alloc processing, state sizes, swap atomicity)

All tests use Result-returning signatures (no unwrap/expect) per project
conventions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test: add comprehensive corruption validity tests for uselesskey-core-negative (#173)

Add 37 integration tests in corruption_validity.rs covering:

- All CorruptPem variants produce structurally invalid PEM (validated
  by checking header/footer mismatch, bad base64 chars, blank lines)
- Truncated DER outputs are exact byte-prefixes of the original
- flip_byte is involutory (double-flip restores original) with XOR 0x01
- Corruption works across all common PEM label types (RSA, EC, CERT, etc.)
- Deterministic corruption is isolated per-variant (no cross-talk)
- Deterministic stability verified across 1000 repeated calls
- CorruptPem::Truncate operates on chars, not bytes (Unicode safety)
- Each variant preserves untouched PEM sections (header/body/footer)
- DER corruption never exceeds original length
- All five PEM and all three DER deterministic arms are reachable
- Edge cases: zero/exact/beyond truncation, single-byte DER, two-byte DER

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add insta snapshot tests for JWK-related crates (#188)

Add snapshot tests for five JWK crates that previously lacked them:

- uselesskey-core-jwk-shape: 12 tests covering all JWK struct types
  (RSA/EC/OKP/Oct public and private), enum variants, Debug redaction
  verification, JWKS serialization, and Display roundtrip
- uselesskey-core-jwks-order: 7 tests covering KidSorted lexicographic
  ordering, stable tie-breaking, mixed duplicates, single/empty/reverse
- uselesskey-core-jwk-builder: 7 tests covering JwksBuilder sorting,
  stable duplicate kids, mixed public/private, add/push methods, empty
  builder, and serialized JWKS shape
- uselesskey-core-jwk: 4 tests verifying facade re-exports, JwksBuilder
  ordering, serialized output, and type availability
- uselesskey-jwk: 4 tests verifying public facade builder ordering,
  mixed key types, AnyJwk conversion, and Display roundtrip

All cryptographic key material (n, e, d, p, q, dp, dq, qi, x, y, k)
is redacted with [REDACTED] in snapshots.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add insta snapshot tests for X.509 crates (#191)

Add snapshot tests for four X.509-related crates that were missing them:

- uselesskey-core-x509: facade re-exports, negative-policy application,
  spec defaults, key-usage stable bytes, derive constants
- uselesskey-core-x509-derive: deterministic base-time derivation,
  serial number generation, boundary safety, constants
- uselesskey-core-x509-negative: variant metadata, applied spec output,
  chain-negative variant names and applied specs
- uselesskey-core-rustls-pki: self-signed/chain/RSA/ECDSA/Ed25519
  PKI type conversion metadata (DER lengths, source matching)

All tests capture metadata only. Key material and certificate bytes
are never included in snapshots. Added insta + serde dev-dependencies
to each crate's Cargo.toml.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test: add security invariant tests for no-key-leakage (#168)

* test: add security invariant tests for no-key-leakage

Add comprehensive tests verifying the no-key-leakage design invariant:

Core tests (crates/uselesskey-core/tests/security_invariants.rs):
- Seed Debug always outputs 'Seed(**redacted**)'
- Factory Debug never contains seed hex bytes or cached values
- Mode::Deterministic Debug shows redacted seed
- Error messages don't echo raw input
- Panic messages from type mismatches don't leak seeds

Integration tests (tests/security_invariants.rs):
- RSA, ECDSA, Ed25519: Debug doesn't contain base64 private/public key bytes
- HMAC: Debug doesn't contain base64 secret bytes or hex prefix
- Token: Debug doesn't contain token value for all spec variants
- All key types: Debug never contains PEM headers
- Generated key bytes not found in committed source files
- Debug output length is bounded (no accidental key dumps)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: resolve clippy warnings in security invariant tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Make skill action schemas automation-ready with required data fields (#3548)

* Make add-comment risk dynamic based on content and confidence

Previously, all add-comment actions were hardcoded as 'high' risk regardless
of content. This change makes the risk level dynamic:

- low: factual content (repro findings, version matrix) with confidence >= 0.85
- medium: workaround/suggestion content with confidence >= 0.85
- high: close/reject content, or confidence < 0.70

Updated in both triage and repro schema cheatsheets and schema definitions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Make skill action schemas automation-ready with required data fields

- Add stateReason, category, milestone fields to both schemas
- Remove update-project (not automatable)
- Add Action Data Requirements section to repro cheatsheet
- Document required fields for every action type in both cheatsheets
- Prevents hollow actions (correct type but missing data)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add comprehensive fault injection & FMEA acceptance tests (#167)

Add 55 acceptance tests for the safety system covering:

1. Safety state transitions (SafeTorque→Challenge→AwaitingAck→Active→Faulted→SafeTorque)
2. Invalid state transitions rejected (wrong tokens, bad states, double-fault)
3. Fault detection within ≤10ms (SAFE-03)
4. Fault response within ≤50ms to safe state (SAFE-04)
5. Watchdog feed/miss/arm/disarm behavior (7 tests)
6. Multi-fault scenarios — rapid succession, sequential, cycling (5 tests)
7. Recovery paths — basic, torque restoration, interlock clear (5 tests)
8. Safety interlock challenge-response — tokens, expiry, cancel, wrong token (5 tests)
9. Torque output limiting — safe mode, high torque, faulted, negative, emergency (7 tests)
10. Determinism — same sequence same result, idempotent clamping (5 tests)

All tests use Result-returning pattern (no unwrap/expect).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(infra): re-enable linear sync workflow with api fixes (#12)

Re-enable the linear-sync.yml workflow that was previously disabled.
Fixed GraphQL API calls to use issues(filter:) instead of issue(id:)
to properly lookup issues by identifier (GHT-XXX).

Changes:
- Renamed linear-sync.yml.disabled back to linear-sync.yml
- Fixed issue lookup to use identifier filter instead of direct ID
- Added error handling for missing issues and API failures
- Changed curl -sf to curl -s with graceful error handling
- Added continue-on-error to linear sync steps
- Workflow triggers on push to GHT-XXX branches and PR events
- Auto-moves issues to In Progress on push, Done on merge
- Posts commit/PR details as comments on Linear issues

Refs: GHT-40

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Prevent install before init with user-friendly error and hints (#228)

* Initial plan

* Prevent install before init with user-friendly error and hints

When `wasm install` is called without a local `wasm.toml` manifest,
abort early with a clear error message and two help hints:
- Suggest running `wasm init` to create the manifest
- Suggest running `wasm registry fetch <component>` as an alternative

Adds spec requirement r[install.no-manifest] and updates the existing
test snapshot to match the new error format.

Co-authored-by: yoshuawuyts <2467194+yoshuawuyts@users.noreply.github.com>
